116.question: natural treatments for sleep apnea Are there ways to prevent sleep apnea or treat it naturally? The patient is looking for natural remedies to prevent and treat sleep apnea.
116.answer: There are natural ways to help manage sleep apnea [PMID:22014867]. Some complementary and integrative approaches, like lifestyle programs, may help reduce sleep apnea [PMID:37532368]. Weight loss and lifestyle programs seem to be the most favorable integrative methods to combine with conventional OSA therapies [PMID:37532368]. Conventional therapies for obstructive sleep apnea (OSA), including CPAP and oral appliances, offer the best opportunity for symptomatic improvement and reduction in OSA overall health impact [PMID:3753

sentence_judgments.sentence: This purpose of this case study is to describe a natural method to help in management of obstructive sleep apnea syndrome (OSAS), which is known to be a common and debilitating condition.
Complementary and integrative approaches to OSA management are varied and, in conjunction with conventional methods, may offer some reduction in the apnea-hypopnea index.
Conventional therapies for obstructive sleep apnea (OSA), including CPAP and oral appliances, offer the best opportunity for symptomatic improvement and reduction in OSA overall health impact.
With rising obesity rates, weight loss and lifestyle programs seem to be the most favorable integrative methods to combine with conventional OSA therapies.

118.question: cornea injury healing How long does a minor cornea injury take to heal without medical attention? A patient believes his cornea injury does not need medical attention. The patient expects the injury to heal on its own, but asking for the time at which the eye should heal.
118.answer: A minor cornea injury usually heals within 48 hours without complications [PMID:25140844]. A focused history and physical exam can identify patients with corneal abrasions and improve intervention time [PMID:25140844]. Corneal abrasion is a common eye injury that occurs in all age-groups [PMID:25140844]. Disruptions in the protective epithelial and stromal layers of the cornea can render the eye susceptible to infection, stromal ulceration, perforation, scarring, and significant vision loss [PMID:

sentence_judgments.sentence: Minor corneal abrasions usually heal within 48 hours without complications or eye damage.
A focused history and physical exam can identify patients with corneal abrasions and improve intervention time.
Corneal abrasion is a common eye injury that occurs in all age-groups.
Disruptions in the protective epithelial and stromal layers of the cornea can render the eye susceptible to infection, stromal ulceration, perforation, scarring, and significant vision loss.

Persistent corneal epithelial defects (PEDs or PCEDs) result from the failure of rapid re-epithelialization and closure within 10-14 days after a corneal injury, even with standard supportive treatment.

Recently-developed medical treatments can target the re-epithelialization process by facilitating access to growth factors and anti-inflammatory agents, and novel surgical techniques can provide re-innervation to the cornea.
After trauma, the cornea typically re-epithelializes promptly thereby minimizing the risk of infection, opacification or perforation.
In the case of deeper corneal injury and corneal pathology the wound healing cascade can also extend to the corneal stroma, the layer below the epithelium.
Although significant progress has been made in recent years, pharmaco-therapeutic agents that promote corneal healing remain limited.

120.question: causes of facial numbness What causes left sided facial numbness? This patient is feeling the left side of his face is numb. He did not have any injuries or procedures, and feels well otherwise. He is concerned this might be a sign of a problem and wants to know if the causes need medical attention.
120.answer: Facial numbness can have several causes, and it's understandable to be concerned [PMID:31471563]. One possibility is numb chin syndrome, which is an uncommon but recognized symptom in medical oncology [PMID:18280947]. This syndrome can manifest as numbness in the lip and chin, sometimes accompanied by warmth in the ear [PMID:18280947]. A case series of 14 patients with facial numbness primarily presented to the ophthalmology clinic [PMID:3147156

sentence_judgments.sentence: To report a case series of 14 patients presenting with facial numbness primarily to the ophthalmology clinic.
Numb chin syndrome is almost unknown within the dental and oral and maxillofacial community, despite being well reported in the medical literature.
Numb chin syndrome, sometimes called numb lip syndrome, is an uncommon but well-recognized symptom in medical oncology.
We report a case of a 79-year-old woman with widespread metastatic disease secondary to breast carcinoma who presented with a left-sided numb lip and chin.
The patient first developed sudden numbness of his left lip and warmth in left ear which travelled to the rest of left face.
This 37-year-old man presented with left sided facial warmth and numbness associated with new sudden-onset right hemiparesis.
No acute infarcts were found on MRI.

122.question: t wave abnormality What is a t wave abnormality? This patient had an ECG test. She was told some t wave abnormalities were seen in the test. She is looking for a definition of t wave abnormalities and an explanation of their meaning.
122.answer: A T wave abnormality is when the T wave on your ECG looks unusual [PMID:11728347]. The causes of abnormal T waves on electrocardiography are multiple and varied [PMID:24276094]. Careful clinical history taking and physical examination are necessary for accurate identification of the cause of such abnormalities [PMID:24276094]. ST segment and T wave changes are the most common ECG abnormalities [PMID:4054634]. Notched or bifid T waves have been reported in a

sentence_judgments.sentence: The causes of abnormal T waves on electrocardiography are multiple and varied.
We present two cases of T wave inversions with markedly different aetiologies.
Careful clinical history taking and physical examination are necessary for accurate identification of the cause of such abnormalities.
ST segment and T wave changes are the most common ECG abnormalities.
Notched or bifid T waves have been reported in a variety of syndromes.
Interpretation must be correlated with other clinical and laboratory information to avoid overestimating or underestimating their significance.
The T-wave abnormality criterion was based on a new set of normal T-wave amplitude limits differentiated by gender, age, electrocardiographic lead, and QRS axis.
Thirteen categories of T-wave abnormalities were tested prospectively.
Quantitative T-wave analysis in an electrocardiographic core laboratory revealed 6 of 13 prespecified categories of T-wave abnormalities that were significantly associated with an adverse outcome.

124.question: cad without angina What does cad without angina mean? This patient was diagnosed with coronary heart disease without angina pectoris. He would like to know more about this condition.
124.answer: CAD without angina means that a patient has coronary heart disease, but they don't experience chest pain (angina pectoris) [PMID:14963655]. This is common, but the prognosis is still being studied [PMID:34487597]. Some patients with CAD and no angina may have a different outcome than those with significant CAD and angina [PMID:17873528]. The absence of obstructive CAD in patients with angina is common, but its prognosis is debated [PMID:3448

sentence_judgments.sentence: Coronary artery disease (CAD) is one of the most common diseases that accounts for a considerable part of health care reimbursement.
Thus, noninvasive diagnosis of CAD is likewise of pivotal importance for medical care today.
The absence of obstructive coronary artery disease (CAD) in patients with angina is common, but its prognosis is debated.
We investigated outcomes of such patients to identify predictors of cardiovascular events.

Limited information is available comparing the clinical characteristics and prognosis for patients with coronary vasospastic angina in the absence of hemodynamically significant coronary artery disease (CAD) (defined as >50% stenosis) versus patients with significant fixed CAD presenting with either stable angina pectoris (SAP) or acute coronary syndromes (ACS).

126.question: nondisjunction What is nondisjunction and what causes it? A family that is considering IVF has heard about nondisjunction. They would like to know more about non-disjunction and its causes.
126.answer: Nondisjunction is when chromosomes don't separate properly during cell division [PMID:9801867]. This can happen in the egg or sperm cells [PMID:9801867]. It's a complex issue, and the exact causes are still being researched [PMID:9801867]. Most cases of aneuploidy, like trisomy 8, are thought to be caused by nondisjunction in the maternal germ line [PMID:19828553]. Linkage studies suggest that abnormal

sentence_judgments.sentence: Causes of chromosomal nondisjunction is one of the remaining unanswered questions in human genetics.
Only two cases from spontaneous abortions were due to maternal meiotic nondisjunction.
In order to increase our understanding of the mechanisms underlying nondisjunction we have performed a molecular study on trisomy 8 and trisomy 8 mosaicism.
It is known that most aneuploidies are due to non-disjunction events occurring in the maternal germ line.
Linkage studies have associated abnormal patterns of meiotic recombination to the origin of the non-disjunction event in many aneuploid conditions.
However, to date, little information on aneuploidy frequency in oocytes from donors is available.
To investigate whether the success rate of ICSI is (1) related to the etiology of infertility or (2) adversely affected by a family history of potential genetic disorders.

128.question: cbd effect on liver enzymes How does CBD effect liver enzymes? This patient would like to know if cannabidiol (CBD) may damage his liver and if the liver enzyme tests will show the effect of CBD on the liver.
128.answer: CBD may affect liver enzymes, and some studies have shown liver enzyme elevations [PMID:36912195]. This could indicate potential liver damage [PMID:36912195]. CBD can cause hepatotoxicity, possibly of a cholestatic nature [PMID:31052254]. The involvement of multiple pathways raises concerns about drug interactions and safety [PMID:31052254]. CBD was rapidly absorbed in all groups, regardless of hepatic function [PMID:30921490

sentence_judgments.sentence: Findings of liver enzyme elevations in recent cannabidiol studies have raised concerns over liver safety.
This study aimed to determine the association between cannabidiol use, liver enzyme elevation, and drug-induced liver injury (DILI).
The involvement of numerous pathways associated with lipid and xenobiotic metabolism raises serious concerns about potential drug interactions as well as the safety of CBD.
In conclusion, CBD exhibited clear signs of hepatotoxicity, possibly of a cholestatic nature.
The goal of this study was to investigate Cannabidiol (CBD) hepatotoxicity in 8-week-old male B6C3F1 mice.

The pharmacokinetics and safety of a single oral dose of 200-mg plant-derived pharmaceutical formulation of highly purified cannabidiol (CBD) in oral solution (Epidiolex in the United States; 100 mg/mL) were assessed in subjects with mild to severe hepatic impairment (n = 8 each for mild and moderate, n = 6 for severe) relative to matched subjects with normal hepatic function (n = 8).
Exposure to the CBD metabolites (6-hydroxy-CBD and 7-hydroxy-CBD) also increased in subjects with moderate and severe hepatic impairment, but to a lesser extent than the parent drug.
CBD was rapidly absorbed in all groups independent of hepatic function (median time to maximum plasma concentration, 2-2.8 hours).

130.question: pcos and oxidative stress Is pcos linked to oxidative stress? The patient with polycystic ovary syndrome has heard the condition is linked to oxidative stress. She would like to know how oxidative stress effects pcos.
130.answer: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is often linked to increased oxidative stress [PMID:37337194]. Oxidative stress is now recognized to play a central role in the pathophysiology of many different disorders, including PCOS [PMID:31198521]. A genetic, epigenetic, and environmental association exists between oxidative stress (OS) and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), expressed in a multifaceted clinical profile [PMID:36279177]. This review summarizes and discusses the role of OS in the pathogenesis of

sentence_judgments.sentence: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is often accompanied by increased oxidative stress levels; however, it is still unclear whether PCOS itself is causally related to oxidative stress (OS), whether OS can increase the occurrence of PCOS, and which characteristics of PCOS increase OS levels.
Therefore, this study explored the causal relationship between PCOS, its characteristics, and OS.
A genetic, epigenetic, and environmental association exists between oxidative stress (OS) and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), expressed in a multifaceted clinical profile.
This review summarizes and discusses the role of OS in the pathogenesis of PCOS syndrome, focusing on metabolic, reproductive, and cancer complications.
Oxidative stress is now recognized to play a central role in the pathophysiology of many different disorders, including PCOS.
This article reviews the literature data related to the mechanisms of oxidative stress in PCOS.

Although intracellular reactive oxygen species (ROS) production and propagation are controlled by highly complex antioxidant enzymatic and non-enzymatic systems, understanding of mechanisms that oxidative stress is important to develop strategies for prevention and therapy of PCOS.

132.question: low vitamin D What is the effect of low vitamin D? The patient diagnosed with vitamin D deficiency would like to know how the problem affects his health.
132.answer: Vitamin D deficiency can affect your health in many ways [PMID:23542507]. It can increase your risk for a wide range of illnesses [PMID:23542507]. Low vitamin D can cause bone pain, muscle weakness, falls, and fractures [PMID:21984863]. It can also lead to osteomalacia, a softening of the bones [PMID:21984863]. Vitamin D deficiency can be caused by medications like anticonvulsants [PMID:2

sentence_judgments.sentence: Vitamin D deficiency and insufficiency as a global health problem are likely to be a risk for wide spectrum of acute and chronic illnesses.
Optimal vitamin D intake and its status are important not only for bone and calcium-phosphate metabolism, but also for overall health and well-being.
A disruption in any part of the vitamin D physiological pathway can result in vitamin D deficiency, which may lead to bone pain, muscle weakness, falls, low bone mass, and fractures.
Physicians should suspect osteomalacia in patients who have prolonged vitamin D deficiency, a low serum calcium level, or a low serum phosphorus level.
Patients may experience abnormal vitamin D metabolism as a result of taking anticonvulsants and other medications.
It is possible or even likely that low vitamin D status increases risk for a multitude of other conditions.
Low vitamin D status is extremely common worldwide due to low dietary intake and low skin production.
Suboptimal vitamin D status contributes to many conditions, including osteomalacia/rickets, osteoporosis, falls, and fractures.

134.question: ibs symptoms and treatments What are the symptoms and treatments for IBS? The patient believes his symptoms may be related to irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). He would like to know more about the symptoms and treatment options.
134.answer: Irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) is a complex functional bowel disorder [PMID:28224836]. Symptoms can include chronic abdominal pain and altered bowel habits [PMID:30466670]. The Rome IV classification groups patients based on predominant stool pattern [PMID:30466670]. There is no definitive treatment for IBS, and recommended treatments focus on symptom relief and improved quality of life [PMID:34128613]. Targeted symptomatic relief for the patient's predominant symptoms provides relief [

sentence_judgments.sentence: Irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) is present in patients with symptoms of chronic abdominal pain and altered bowel habits but no identifiable organic etiology.
Targeted symptomatic relief for the patient's predominant symptoms provides relief in addition to effective older medications that are inexpensive and reliable.
Rome IV classification groups patients based on predominant stool pattern.

This article aims to inform readers about the symptoms and sub-classifications of IBS and the range of pharmacological and non-pharmacological treatments available, to enable nurses to understand and manage symptoms of the condition in this group of patients.
Irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) is a complex functional bowel disorder.
IBS is often misdiagnosed, and is not always managed effectively, despite the guidance available to clinicians.
There is no definitive treatment for IBS, and recommended treatments focus on symptom relief and improved quality of life.
Irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) is a heterogeneous group of conditions related to specific biologic and cellular abnormalities that are not fully understood.
Trusting patient-physician interactions are essential to help patients understand and accept an IBS diagnosis and to actively engage in effective self-management.

136.question: treatment for elbow pain What can be done for severe elbow pain? The patient's elbow started hurting after playing tennis. The pain was not relieved by Aleve. The patient would like to know what else could be done.
136.answer:
Severe elbow pain after playing tennis can be frustrating, especially when over-the-counter pain relievers like Aleve don't help [PMID:1419555]. It's important to get a proper diagnosis from a doctor or physical therapist to rule out other possible causes of your pain [PMID:1419555].

Physical therapy can be very helpful for tennis elbow [PMID:36975037]. A physical therapist can guide you through exercises to strengthen the muscles in your wrist and forearm [PMID:3

sentence_judgments.sentence: There are still many open questions surrounding the syndrome of tennis elbow.
Tennis elbow is a common overuse syndrome.
It may be best diagnosed clinically by eliminating other possible causes of lateral elbow pain.

Here, we explain what the guidelines say about how to diagnose tennis elbow (sometimes called lateral elbow pain or lateral elbow tendinopathy), the specific treatments your physical therapist might offer you to help manage pain and improve the mobility of your elbow, and the types of exercises you could do to strengthen the muscles in your wrist and forearm.
Experts in managing elbow pain developed a guideline for rehabilitation clinicians to use to help you understand more about tennis elbow.
Most people with tennis elbow feel pain on the outside of their elbow and upper forearm.
Tennis elbow is largely a self-limiting condition.
The principles of therapy for tennis elbow are to relieve pain, microbleeding and inflammation, promote healing, rehabilitate the injured arm and try to prevent recurrence.

Making sense of the literature on the treatment of tennis elbow is difficult because there are few studies that have used the acceptable epidemiological techniques of the prospective randomised controlled trial or case-controlled study.

138.question: Sudafed Afrin interaction Can I use Sudafed after using Afrin for nasal congestion? Afrin did not relieve nasal congestion in the patient who is now wondering if taking Sudafed will help. The patient would like to know if it is safe to take the two medications together.
138.answer: It's understandable that you're looking for relief from nasal congestion [PMID:34607935]. Afrin, or oxymetazoline, can cause rebound congestion if used for more than 3 days [PMID:32628388]. Therefore, it's best to limit your use of Afrin to 3 days [PMID:32628388]. Using Afrin for more than 3 days can lead to rhinitis medicamentosa, a condition where your nose becomes congested from the medication itself [

sentence_judgments.sentence: To prevent the development of rebound nasal congestion caused by topical decongestants, it is important to limit the frequency of their use.

However, in patients who have had a history of episodes of rebound nasal congestion, which develops including the previously indicated periods, the duration of decongestant therapy should be limited to 3 days.
The most common rebound nasal congestion associated with the use of topical decongestants.
Long-term use of decongestant nasal spray (alpha adrenergic agonist) causes nasal congestion by rhinitis medicamentosa.
The over-the-counter nasal decongestant oxymetazoline (eg, Afrin) is used in the pediatric population for a variety of conditions in the operating room setting.

Current US Food and Drug Administration approval for oxymetazoline is for patients ≥6 years of age, but medical professionals may elect to use it short-term and off label for younger children in particular clinical scenarios in which the potential benefit may outweigh risks (eg, active bleeding, acute respiratory distress from nasal obstruction, acute complicated sinusitis, improved surgical visualization, nasal decongestion for scope examination, other conditions, etc).

In the urgent care setting, emergency department, or inpatient setting, to avoid excessive administration of the medication, medical professionals should use the spray bottle in an upright position with the child upright.

140.question: spontaneous hand fractures What can cause acute fractures in both of your hands when there hasn’t been injury or trauma? A patient felt pain in his hands after vigorous exercise. In the ER, he was told he has fractures. He is wondering what could have caused the fractures: he did not have any injuries during exercise.
140.answer: Spontaneous hand fractures can occur without any apparent injury [PMID:19275452]. These fractures can happen in seemingly normal bone [PMID:19275452]. Some possible causes include vigorous exercise [PMID:19275452]. Seizures, whether epileptic or non-epileptic, can also lead to fractures [PMID:20205769]. Metabolic conditions that decrease bone mineral density can also cause fractures [PMID:20205769]. In some

sentence_judgments.sentence: Spontaneous fracture occurs primarily in two distinct groups of patients: the very active young and the elderly.
Spontaneous fractures occur in seemingly normal bone with no apparent blunt-force trauma.

Researchers and clinicians have used several terms interchangeably for spontaneous fracture, including pathologic fracture, fragility fracture, compression fracture, or fatigue or insufficiency fracture.
Seizures with or without trauma may cause fractures that occur commonly in epileptic seizures.
Fracture risk is less reported in non-epileptic seizures.
Some metabolic conditions leading to a decrease in bone mineral density may cause fractures secondary to non-epileptic seizure.
Workup revealed no underlying explanation for the fractures.
To our knowledge, this has not been reported.
An appropriate workup should identify most of these underlying conditions.

142.question: Medications that raise blood pressure What medication has a drug interaction with high blood pressure? The hypertensive patient is concerned that some of the medications he is taking may elevate his blood pressure and interact with his antihypertensive medications.
142.answer: Hypertensive patients often take multiple medications for other health conditions [PMID:2182265]. This can lead to drug interactions that raise blood pressure [PMID:2182265]. Clinicians need to be aware of these interactions to avoid unwanted side effects [PMID:2182265]. The use of antihypertensive medications combined with polypharmacy increases the risk of drug interactions [PMID:33666764]. If a patient's blood pressure rises suddenly, it's important to consider medications that could

sentence_judgments.sentence: Hypertensive patients may have other medical illnesses for which they receive medications, and interactions between antihypertensive agents and other drugs is likely.

Clinicians should be aware of the mechanisms and the consequences of the different types of interaction in hypertensive patients, so that a desired pharmacological response can be achieved with the fewest side effects in the patients.
Some of these interactions may lead to undesirable effects or even loss of blood pressure control.
The pervasive use of antihypertensive medications combined with the presence of polypharmacy in many hypertensive patients results in significant risk of drug interactions.
This review will summarize the relevant literature to assist clinicians in mitigating drug interaction risks when prescribing antihypertensives.
It often requires the use of multi-drug regimens and is commonly associated with a myriad of other comorbidities which increase medication use.
When a patient with well controlled hypertension is presented with acute blood pressure elevation, use of drug or chemical substance which increases blood pressure should be suspected.
We summarize the therapeutic agents or chemical substances that elevate blood pressure and their mechanisms of action.
Drug-induced blood pressure increases are usually minor and short-lived, although rare hypertensive emergencies associated with use of certain drugs have been reported.

144.question: starting hypertension treatment When should I treat high blood pressure? The patient has high blood pressure, but otherwise is healthy and fit. She is wondering if she should talk to her doctor about treatment options.
144.answer: It's good that you're thinking about managing your high blood pressure [PMID:16463797]. The general recommendation is to treat high blood pressure if it's consistently high, like 140/190 mmHg [PMID:16463797]. Hypertension is a common condition that can lead to serious problems like heart attack and stroke [PMID:17269288]. If you have diabetes or kidney disease, you might need treatment sooner [PMID:1646379

sentence_judgments.sentence: Having blood pressures 140/190 mmHg repeatedly at the doctor's office is associated with an increased cardiovascular risk and requires drug treatment if non-pharmacological measures do not allow blood pressure normalization.
There is no doubt today that hypertensive therapy is effective in preventing cardiovascular complications.
Antihypertensive therapy might already be required in patients exhibiting simultaneously blood pressure > or = 130/180 mmHg and diabetes and/or renal disease.

Dentists must also be cognizant of the implications high blood pressure has for dental practice.
Hypertension is a common malady and a harbinger of such diseases as heart attack and stroke.
High blood pressure should be treated gradually.
However, physicians should confirm blood pressure readings and appropriately classify the hypertensive state.

When patients present with severely elevated blood pressure (i.e., systolic blood pressure of 180 mm Hg or greater, or diastolic blood pressure of 110 mm Hg or greater), physicians need to differentiate hypertensive emergency from severely elevated blood pressure without signs or symptoms of end-organ damage (severe asymptomatic hypertension).

146.question: Management of PTSD How can PTSD be treated/managed? A war veteran is seeking help for PTSD.
146.answer: Posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD) is an uncomfortable response that can follow exposure to 1 or more dangerous or frighteningly traumatic circumstances [PMID:34000134]. Most combat veterans with PTSD at Veterans Administration medical centers in the United States are prescribed pharmacotherapy [PMID:34000134]. This narrative review describes psychotherapeutic and pharmacologic approaches to treat PTSD [PMID:34000134]. Trauma-focused psychological therapies should be offered as the first line of treatment for post-traumatic stress disorder [PMID

sentence_judgments.sentence: Posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD) is an uncomfortable response that can follow exposure to 1 or more dangerous or frighteningly traumatic circumstances.
Most combat veterans with PTSD at Veterans Administration medical centers in the United States are prescribed pharmacotherapy.
This narrative review describes psychotherapeutic and pharmacologic approaches to treat PTSD.
Before a diagnosis of PTSD can be made, the patient's symptoms must significantly disrupt normal activities and last for more than one month.
The lifetime prevalence of PTSD in the United States is 8 to 9 percent, and approximately 25 to 30 percent of victims of significant trauma develop PTSD.
Although post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) is a debilitating anxiety disorder that may cause significant distress and increased use of health resources, the condition often goes undiagnosed.
Trauma-focused psychological therapies should be offered as the first line of treatment for post-traumatic stress disorder.

GPs may be seeing more patients with post-traumatic stress disorder as military personnel return from overseas deployments.

148.question: easy bruising Why do I bruise so easily? A 59-year-old woman noticed multiple spontaneous bruises in the past several weeks. She would like to know what causes this bruising.
148.answer: Many people experience easy bruising, and it's often nothing to worry about [PMID:16634245]. However, it's important to understand why you might be bruising more easily [PMID:16634245]. The physical examination should evaluate evidence of bleeding and identify systemic illness [PMID

150.answer: Many people with lumbar disc herniation seek physical therapy [PMID:27765714]. It is unclear whether physical therapy is effective for this population [PMID:27765714]. sentence_judgments.sentence: Physical therapy is commonly sought by people with lumbar disc herniation and associated radiculopathy.

154.question: breathing techniques for managing shortness of breath How to breathe when you are short of breath? The patient is constantly out of breath and wants to improve breathing using breathing techniques and exercise.
154.answer: Breathing techniques can help manage shortness of breath [PMID:18686398]. These techniques aim to improve breathing control and quality of life [PMID:18686398]. Breathing exercises can be used to control asthma symptoms [PMID:32212422]. Breathing exercises include tidal and minute volume training, relaxation, and modification of breathing patterns [PMID:32212422]. Breathing exercises can be performed as the Papworth Method, the Buteyko breathing technique, yogic breathing, or

sentence_judgments.sentence: Living with breathlessness can have a considerable impact on a person's quality of life, but even slight improvements in breathing control have significant benefits for patients.
This article explores different breathing control techniques which can help clinicians working in primary care to support these patients.
Breathlessness is a common but complex symptom which has a wide range of causes and presentations.

The training of breathing usually focuses on tidal and minute volume and encourages relaxation, exercise at home, the modification of breathing pattern, nasal breathing, holding of breath, lower rib cage and abdominal breathing.
Breathing exercises have been widely used worldwide as a non-pharmacological therapy to treat people with asthma.
